texto: We may take it that the world is undoubtedly itself [i.e., is indistinct from itself], but, in any attempt to see itself, as an object, it must, equally, undoubtedly act so as to make itself distinct from and therefore false to itself.In this sense, in respect to its own information, the universe must expand to escape the telescopes through which we, who are it, are trying to capture it, which is us.
explicacao: Orientação direta de G. Spencer Brown sobre temas ligados a autoestima. A afirmação “We may take it that the world is undoubtedly itself [i.e., is indistinct from itself], but, in an…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a autoestima, cosmology, identity, útil em momentos de identity e reflection.
